messages.content: Make a story of a teenager, where suddenly, his hair and eye color represented the current composition of U.S. politics. His hair color represents the party control of presidency, left eye is the U.S. House control, and the right one as U.S. Senate control. It was in January 20th, 2009. He woke up all of a sudden with this. The character is 14 years old at the time. Democrats had a trifecta, and the character suddenly gained immortality and knowledge of politics.

At 14, Lucas had no particular interest in politics; his head was filled with dreams of becoming a star soccer player, or maybe a videogame developer. But on January 20th, 2009, a strange thing happened, something that would alter the path of his young life in ways he could never have predicted. Lucas went to bed the night before with his usual ashy brown hair and plain hazel eyes but awoke to find himself looking startlingly different.

It was Inauguration Day, and the United States was on the brink of the Obama presidency. The air buzzed with change, and somehow, inexplicably, the seismic shift in the country's political landscape had manifested in Lucas' very being. His hair, once nondescript, was now a deep and vibrant blue, the same blue that blanketed maps on the election night to denote Democratic strongholds.

He stumbled to the bathroom mirror in disbelief, squinting at his reflection. As he gazed into his eyes, he saw that his left iris had transformed into the same bold blue, representing the Democratic control of the U.S. House of Representatives. Meanwhile, his right iris mirrored this with an equally intense blue, indicating the Democratic majority in the Senate. The colors were rich and unmistakable; Lucas was a walking, breathing symbol of a political trifecta.

Along with his startling new look, Lucas discovered another phenomenon; his mind was now filled with an encyclopedic understanding of the American political system, its history, and its intricate dynamics. He could recount election results, name every senator and member of Congress, and analyze policy with the acuity of a seasoned pundit.

More incredible, though, was the dawning realization that he was now seemingly immortal. As days passed without a hair on his head changing—or a single blue strand falling to the ground—Lucas realized that his body was no longer aging. This strange magic tied his fate to the ebbs and flows of U.S. politics.

The years went by, and Lucas' hair and eyes became a curious barometer of the shifting political tides. In a country divided by red and blue, he was a unique patchwork symbolizing unity or division, depending on the balance of power. People would glance nervously at his hair or peer into his eyes to guess what the latest election had brought, before even checking the news.

With elections happening every two years for Congress and every four for the presidency, Lucas' hues shifted—the red of Republican victories, the blue of Democratic triumphs. Each election night, he'd feel a tingling sensation, and by morning, his appearance would match the new political landscape. Lucas became a public figure, watched and analyzed, a living political forecast.

But beyond the curiosity and fame, Lucas grew wise beyond his years. His immortality brought him not just unending youth but a sense of responsibility. He used his knowledge to educate, to bridge gaps, and to remind those around him that the colors of politics are fleeting, but the nation they represent must stand united.

As Lucas journeyed through the decades, a timeless observer of the democratic process, he pondered the whims of fate that had chosen him for this strange and extraordinary life. He was a mosaic of his country's politics, a silent testament to the ever-changing heart of America, casting his eternal youthful gaze upon an evolving nation.
Story, January 2009 to May 2010.

Lucas Hartwell was on the cusp of ordinary teenage life; his world was one of homework, skateboards, and a budding interest in music. But on the morning of January 20th, 2009, Lucas awoke to a life that was anything but ordinary. When he gazed into the mirror, his shock was palpable. His hair was now a startling shade of blue, rich and glossed, as if imbued with the spirit of the Democratic Party which had just seized control of the executive branch. Each eye, too, had transformed, now reflecting the dual shade of Democratic blue—one for the House, one for the Senate.

Worse, Lucas couldn't hide it. His parents were aghast, and it wasn't long before his strange condition drew media attention. Camera crews began camping outside his house; social media dubbed him 'DemoBoy'; and pundits speculated wildly about what his existence might mean. And inexplicably, as knots of confusion and fear started to unravel, Lucas realized he knew precisely why his hair and eyes had changed. Knowledge of American politics from its inception until that very moment filled his mind, though he had no memory of learning it.

With this, he also noticed he wasn't getting any older. Injuries healed overnight, illnesses never came, and his body was a bastion of unyielding youth. Lucas had been granted immortality, tied to the rhythm of political pendulums. He felt the weight of this new reality—he would watch generations pass while he remained the same.

By the spring of 2009, his novelty had become a kind of celebrity. Lucas was invited to talk shows and political panels, where his insights and predictions, drawn from an intuitive and inexplicable understanding of political patterns, were unnervingly accurate. His status as a kind of political oracle was cemented.

When the 2010 mid-term elections approached, his existence—now a commonplace oddity—garnered fresh interest. As the campaigns heated up, so did Lucas's ability to educate and influence. He participated in get-out-the-vote initiatives and became a mascot for democracy, urging people—irrespective of party—to exercise their civic duty.

But as election night rolled around, Lucas felt that now-familiar tingling sensation. He knew what was to come. Eyes of the nation turned to him, awaiting the physical manifestation of the political shift they sensed on the horizon. The election brought a significant sway—Republicans took control of the House while Democrats retained the Senate. As predicted, Lucas woke up the following morning with his left eye changed, not dramatically, but to a lighter blue—the Democrats had lost seats, but still held the majority in the Senate. His right eye, however, swirled into an undeniable shade of red.

Amidst media frenzy and the public's reaction, Lucas stood as a manifestation of a polarized nation—dual-colored eyes marking the split in congressional power. The sheen of his hair, now a blend of red and blue hues, shone under the flashing lights of cameras, signaling that his being was intrinsically tied with the colors of the political map.

From January 2009 to May 2010, Lucas Hartwell lived as a unique witness to history, embodying the triumphs and divisions of the American political landscape. With the entire scope of U.S. politics flickering across his features, Lucas stepped forward into an uncertain future, offering his gift of sight to a nation charting its course through choppy waters. He stood as a reminder that amidst the ever-changing realm of politics, the strength of democracy lies in its enduring nature, outlasting any one color or party. And he, its undying sentinel, would watch it all unfold.
May 2010 to December 2011. He watched the 2010 midterms unfold.

As May 2010 rolled into summer, Lucas' unique condition captured the imagination of the nation. He had become a referendum on the nation's political mood, a walking poll for an electorate polarized and tense. With the 2010 midterms on the horizon, Lucas found himself the subject of intense scrutiny. Politicians from both sides of the political spectrum tried to decipher the future through him, but Lucas remained impartial, a living embodiment of democracy, bound to reflect rather than influence the will of the people.

Lucas had grown accustomed to the media circus, the unyielding barrage of questions, the stares that followed him as he navigated his otherwise typical teen life. His friends joked that he was the ultimate swing voter, but Lucas felt the weight of his immortality. It was a lonely existence, knowing that as others aged, he would remain the perpetual 14-year-old.

By the time November 2010 arrived, the United States was a cauldron of heated political debate, teetering on the brink of a transformative midterm election. Lucas watched as news networks covered the countdown, his unique condition a perpetual backdrop to their coverage. On election night, he stayed up late, his blue eyes glued to the screen, witnessing the real-time shift of political power.

By the end of the night, the results were clear. The Democratic Party had suffered significant losses. The Republicans had taken control of the House of Representatives, while the Democrats maintained a shrunken majority in the Senate. Lucas felt the tingling in his right eye as it morphed into the crimson shade of the Republican Party, betraying the shift in power.

The next day, Lucas looked at himself in the mirror. He was a living dichotomy, blue hair hinting at the still-Democratic presidency, his right eye a deep red for the Republican-controlled House, and his left eye a lighter blue, reflecting the reduced Democratic dominance in the Senate.

As 2011 dawned, America was steeped in division. Lucas, however, found an unexpected role. His immortality and insight made him a sought-after speaker and educator. He toured schools and universities, explaining the workings of Congress, the importance of every election, and the impact of political engagement. He strove to be a unifying figure, showing that beyond the colors that divided them, Americans shared common ground.

Lucas spent the better part of the year reaching out, connecting, and teaching, his very existence a conversation starter. In a world barraged by 24-hour news cycles and saturated with partisan rhetoric, Lucas provided a calm, factual counterpoint, a living reminder of democracy’s permanence.

All the while, Lucas bore witness to the profound political cacophony that raged around him. He watched Occupy Wall Street participants take to the streets, clamoring for economic equality. He observed as the Tea Party continued to reshape the Republican landscape. The world was changing, and he, the immortal teenager, watched its relentless march forward.

By December 2011, as the nation braced itself for the upcoming presidential election year, Lucas Hartwell stood as a monument to the past few years of political shift. His red and blue eyes signaled an era characterized by discontent and discourse, while his hair—a now-fading blue—hinted at President Obama's incumbent presence.

Lucas knew that the next year would again transform him, a testament to the outcome of the people's voice. And although he couldn't age, couldn't vote, couldn't campaign, he fostered with every word and every appearance the essence of democracy—education, understanding, and the power of every single ballot.
January 2012 to March 2013. On April 2012, he turned 16 both chronologically and biologically. His classmates were curious.

As the calendar flipped to January 2012, the political stage was set with President Obama seeking reelection and the Republican party in the midst of its primary season. Lucas, forever marked by the blue hue of his hair but now with politically bifurcated eyes, was a high school sophomore both chronologically and, despite his unique condition, biologically still a teenager. The curiosity about his singular appearance had become a normal part of his daily life, but the prospect of another presidential election meant renewed attention and speculation.

In the lead-up to November, the American political landscape seethed with campaign fervor. Lucas, with his inherent repository of political wisdom and unchanging youth, was more engaged than ever. His high school teachers occasionally invited him to give talks on government and the importance of voting, utilizing his anomaly to captivate the otherwise disinterested teenage crowd.

April 2012 marked Lucas's sixteenth birthday, and with it came an extraordinary event. While he was prepared for the common milestone, what Lucas didn't expect was an actual physical shift. Overnight, his appearance matured slightly—his jawline pronounced, his stature a touch taller, a mirror to his peers. The entrenched magic of his immortality allowed him a single transformation to match his chronological age and then froze him once more. This change sparked fresh whispers and theories among his classmates. Some joked that maybe the political tides had granted him this one rite of passage; others watched him closely, half-expecting his appearance to shift with the polls.

Throughout 2012, Lucas maneuvered his life under the public eye with an awkward grace, balancing being a teenager with his role as a political emblem. When election night came, Lucas could feel a now-familiar prickle of apprehension. The nation was holding its breath, uncertain if his blue hair would fade to red or remain the steely Democrat blue. As states were called and results tallied, Lucas waited. 

By the end of election night, President Obama had secured a second term, reassuring the blue shade of Lucas's hair. The Senate remained under Democratic control though the margin tightened, subtly altering the shade of his left eye. The House stayed firmly red, keeping his right eye a vibrant ruby color. He woke the next morning to a reflection that matched the divided government, a country still split between blue and red.

Throughout 2013, as Lucas entered the latter half of his high school years, he saw his country grapple with its divisions—from legislative battles in Congress to social issues that spilled onto the streets. Lucas found himself at gatherings and debates not just as a spectator, but also as an analyst, his near-endless political knowledge sought after by those trying to understand the past and anticipate the future.

His classmates, initially bewildered by his sudden physical maturation, had accepted his unchanged appearance by March 2013. To them, Lucas was simply Lucas—their peer with an unnatural knack for political foresight and a testament to American democracy. His eternal youth was no longer startling; it was just another aspect of the remarkable teenager they knew.

As he continued to bridge the years, his blue and red eyes watching the world change around him, Lucas Hartwell remained a constant in a sea of change, his life entwined with the heartbeat of the nation. Though rumors of his immortality had quieted, the truth of it stretched out before him—a journey through time, politics, and adolescence, as unique as the colors of his hair and eyes.
and, his other crimson eye also added a shade of black, mirroring GOP gerrymanders.

The year of 2012 stretched not only across the passage of time but also across the fabric of the United States’ political landscape. As Lucas watched the intricate strategies of gerrymandering playing out across various states, a new phenomenon occurred. With the Republican Party solidifying control of the House in part through the redrawing of district lines, his right eye, already a deep Republican red from their hold over the House, swirled with streaks of black shadowing the edges—the visual manifestation of the political machinations at work.

As Lucas stood before the mirror on the morning after the districts had been reshaped, the subtle change was undeniable. The black tendrils weaving through his right eye prompted a flurry of questions and renewed media frenzy around him. People wanted to know what this new development meant, and to many, it signaled a darker aspect of political control—an acknowledgment of the maneuvers that transcended simple majority rule.

As he turned 16, the attention wasn't just on the biological shift in his appearance. His peers, now accustomed to the spectacle that seemed to follow him, were fascinated by the change in his right eye. In history classes, discussions about gerrymandering sparked conversations about the ethicality of such practices. Lucas' unique condition served as a tangible example of how abstract political concepts could manifest in reality. 

His friends at school were a mix of intrigued and concerned. The addition of black to his right eye's crimson prompted debates in the cafeteria and corridors, a real-time lesson in civics woven through their adolescence. Lucas himself, with his growing fatigue regarding his public role, became increasingly reflective about his place in the world. He was both a watchdog and a canvas, symbolizing the consequences of political acts on the nation's soul.

Throughout 2013, Lucas continued to absorb the tensions that came with a deeply divided Congress. The nation struggled with sequester-induced budget cuts and a government shutdown, each political tremor mirrored in the gnarled dance of black and red in his right eye. Despite these challenges, he maintained his determination to use his condition to educate and promote participation in democracy. 

The blackness within his crimson eye was not just a symbol of partisan maneuvering; it reminded those who looked upon Lucas that politics were more than policies and party colors—they were about the lives of the citizens and the health of the country’s political systems. As Lucas evolved in his understanding of his own immortality, he also evolved in his message. He became not just a reflection of politics but a beacon for political integrity and change.

Despite the darkness that seemed to cloud his right eye, Lucas Hartwell remained steadfast. A beacon of unwavering youth set against a backdrop of ever-changing political schemes, Lucas continued to navigate the halls of high school while carrying the weight of his peculiar destiny. The addition of black to his eye was a cautionary tale spun into a singular human experience—a reminder of the vigilance required to preserve the pillars of democracy amidst the shifting tides of political power.
May 2013 to February 2015. He watched the 2014 midterms unfold.

Between May 2013 and February 2015, Lucas Hartwell's life was a curious blend of typical teenage experience and extraordinary political symbolism. He walked the halls of his high school, stuck in the body of a 16-year-old, his unique appearance a constant reminder that he was anything but ordinary.

The black-streaked crimson of Lucas's right eye remained a discussion point, not only in his school but also in political discourse nationwide. It served as a warning of the intricate and sometimes obscured nature of political control exerted behind the scenes. His blue hair for the Presidency and the lighter blue of his left eye indicated a less solidified Democratic control of the Senate, and together, they completed the palette of America's divided political climate.

When the midterm election cycle began to heat up in 2014, Lucas found himself once again thrust into the limelight. His existence had fascinated political scientists and strategists who wondered if his physical changes could indeed predict the outcomes. As redistricting and campaign battles intensified, Lucas's insights–a blend of his own observations and the extensive knowledge that filled his mind since that morning in 2009–were in high demand.

As November approached, the tension in the political atmosphere was almost palpable. Lucas continued his work, engaging with civics groups, speaking at public forums, and using his platform to remind the populace of their power at the polls. The stakes felt especially high this time around as control of the Senate seemed uncertain.

On election night, Lucas sat quietly in the living room of his family's home, the television flashing with maps and percentages. He felt that familiar tingling sensation starting at his left eye. The Republicans were gaining as the results poured in, flipping seat after seat in their favor.

By the end of the night, the GOP had not only retained its majority in the House but had also seized control of the Senate. The blue in Lucas's left eye receded, leaving a pale shade that flickered out and was replaced by a deep red–a reflection of the new balance of power. His eyes were now both tinged with the same stark red, while his hair remained a defiant Democrat blue.

In the wake of the midterm elections, the country looked to Lucas as a prophetic figure, his very appearance a mirror to their choices and the consequences thereof. The two red eyes with one hosting specks of black became the center of talks regarding the potency and reach of party strategies in modern politics.

For Lucas, the months that followed brought a new kind of isolation. His political dichotomy made him an even more prominent public figure, but within the walls of his high school, he still grappled with the ordinary challenges of adolescence, albeit frozen in time. His friends began to plan for college, while Lucas faced the prospect of perpetual youth and an endless road of political ebb and flow.

By February 2015, with the new Congress in session, Lucas watched as a fresh dynamic formed in Washington D.C. The fully Republican-led legislative branches clashed with the Democrat-held Presidency, and his red eyes and blue hair represented an unprecedented level of partisan polarization.

As Lucas navigated the complexities of this era, his immortality and unchanging youth remained a poignant reminder of the constants in American life: change, conflict, and the flow of time. His unique condition also spoke to deeper themes of continuity, resilience, and the hope that democracy, regardless of its struggles and strains, could endure and evolve.
He also became slightly more conservative as a result.

As Lucas's physical appearance reflected the political shifts of the nation, a subtler change began to take root within his psyche — a gradual ideological shift influenced by the very events his unique traits mirrored. 

The spectacle of his dual red eyes and the strands of black entwined within one, both symbolic of the Republican hold on Congress, affected him more than he anticipated. While Lucas had always strived to maintain his impartiality as a living emblem of the country's politics, the constant exposure to Republican rhetoric and policy, along with the responsibilities thrust upon him to understand and explain these shifts, led to a slight conservative tilt in his own thinking.

As he watched the 2014 midterms unfold and witnessed the country's swing to the right, Lucas found the values and arguments of fiscal responsibility, strong national defense, and individual liberties resonating with him. The immersion in political debate and the need to articulate the perspectives underlying the current political climate made him more receptive to certain conservative principles.

Lucas's personal turn toward conservatism startled some of his schoolmates and family, who had known him as a moderate or non-partisan. The change was subtle, not wholesale; he still held firm to many core beliefs that centered on social justice and equity. But he could no longer claim total detachment from the ideologies that colored him physically and, now, philosophically.

The reactions were mixed — some friends at school questioned whether his political transformation was a direct result of his connection to the political shifts, while others suggested it was a natural outcome of being so deeply engrossed in the political process. There were debates in his classrooms, where teachers used Lucas's experience as an example of how exposure to certain ideas might influence one's beliefs, and discussions about the importance of diverse sources and perspectives.

Through these nuanced ideological changes, Lucas learned a valuable lesson about empathy and the complexity of political beliefs. He understood that being the embodiment of the nation's political state meant more than just representing divisions; it was also about the personal journeys that individuals took as they engaged with the ebbs and flows of political tides.

Internally, Lucas grappled with his newfound conservative leanings, aware that his every action was scrutinized as a possible reflection of the nation's political heartbeat. He applied his deep knowledge of American government to explore this shift within himself, seeking a balanced understanding of the array of viewpoints that make up the nation's political discourse.

By February 2015, Lucas's conservative shift had become an integral, though contested, part of his identity. His journey from detached observer to an ideologically invested participant mirrored the larger story of a country continually reinventing itself through the diverse beliefs and values of its people. Amidst the red of his eyes and the deep blue of his hair, Lucas Hartwell was coming to embody not just the political spectrum of the United States but the living, evolving nature of its democracy.
February 2015 to October 2016. In a election year, his hair color turned to desaturated light blue.

As the wheel of time turned from February 2015 to October 2016, Lucas Hartwell found himself in the limelight once again, his unique condition a barometer for America's political climate. As the election cycle progressed, the vibrant Democrat blue of his hair, a holdover from the Obama administration, slowly faded into a desaturated light blue. It was as if the certainty of the incumbent presidency was ebbing away, making room for the unknown.

This spectral change was not lost on Lucas's schoolmates or the media, who both observed that the lightening of his hair's hue possibly portended a weakening grip of the Democratic party on the executive branch. Lucas himself couldn't help but feel a twist of anticipation and a knot of concern as the presidential race unfolded.

The year was a cascade of primaries, caucuses, and debates. Lucas, who by now had a reputation as an insightful analyst, was invited to participate in numerous discussions and interviews. In these engagements, he noted how his slight ideological shift towards conservatism influenced his perception of the candidates' platforms.

As the months inched closer to November, the presidential campaigns intensified. Lucas's life was a whirlwind of speaking engagements and constant media scrutiny. His two deeply red eyes mirrored the Republican stronghold on Congress, but the change in his hair color was a visual cue that highlighted the uncertainty and shifting sands of the upcoming election.

The right eye, with its swirls of black indicating the manipulation behind the red majority, stood testament to the power of strategy over the democratic process. Meanwhile, his classmates witnessed Lucas's personal evolution, his conservative inclinations grounding him amidst the tumult of this election year.

When Donald Trump secured the Republican nomination and Hillary Clinton the Democratic one, the polarization within the country was palpable, and Lucas's desaturated blue hair seemed to absorb the tension. Despite his slight conservative leanings, Lucas found himself often in the middle, an island of neutrality and reason in the sea of partisan rhetoric.

Throughout this time, Lucas continued to engage with his peers, encouraging them to look beyond the colors that represented each party and remember the values and policies at the heart of the elections. He underscored the importance of informed voting and civic participation, all while his own appearance served as a stark visual reminder of the stakes involved.

By October 2016, as the nation leaned in towards the final weeks before the election, Lucas's light blue hair cast a glow of muted uncertainty. It was a reflection of the fading Obama era, the contested nature of the presidential race, and the sense that a dramatic change might be upon the horizon.

Lucas, now a teenager both in appearance and in actual age for several years, was a vessel for the political soul of the U.S., his shifting colors echoing the nation's discord and hopes. Standing at the precipice of a new political era, he was a living testament to the democratic process—a process that, despite its flaws, he hoped would steer the nation toward a more perfect union.
On October 29, his hair turned light red, defying polls, and hinting Trump presidency.

As October 2016 drew to its close, an unexpected change occurred that sent ripples of shock throughout the country. On the 29th, as if splashed by an invisible painter, Lucas's once desaturated blue hair adopted a hue of light red. This sudden transformation, coming just days before the historic election, defied polls and pundit predictions that had largely been forecasting a victory for the Democrats.

Lucas stood before his mirror in disbelief, fingers tentatively brushing through the strands that now hinted at a Republican win. The change was subtle, a pale shade that was hard to discern, but unmistakably red. The metamorphosis entwined with a date that would become infamous in political circles, as it coincided with the FBI's announcement regarding the investigation into Hillary Clinton's use of a private email server.

The media, already abuzz with 24-hour coverage of the election, pounced on Lucas's latest development with fervor. Speculation erupted across television networks, in newspapers, and on social media—was Lucas's condition truly a harbinger for the outcome of the presidential race? Could his hair color indeed be prophetic, or was it merely a bizarre coincidence?

At school, Lucas's classmates were equally perplexed. They had grown up with Lucas's political transformations, and many had come to regard them with a mix of reverence and skepticism. The shift to light red sparked heated discussions, and some students who supported the Democratic candidate viewed it as a bad omen, while others dismissed it as an inexplicable quirk unrelated to the real-world ballot box.

Lucas himself grappled with the change. His slight conservative bent made the new color feel less alien, but he understood the magnitude of what his hair potentially indicated. A Trump presidency had seemed an improbable outcome to many, yet here he was, a silent notification of an electoral upset in the making.

For Lucas, the days leading up to the election were a whirlwind of conflicting emotions. His unique ability to predict political outcomes, previously an intellectual curiosity, now felt heavy with consequence. What did it mean to carry such a sign of division on his very head? He pondered, concerned with the notion that he was somehow at the center of a deeply fractured moment in American history.

When Election Day arrived on November 8th, 2016, both the nation and Lucas held their breath. As the votes were counted and states began to declare their results, the improbable emerged as reality—Donald Trump was elected the 45th President of the United States. Lucas's light red hair was indeed a harbinger of this stunning political shift.

The realization that his appearance could truly reflect and perhaps foreshadow such significant events was both surreal and burdensome for Lucas. As the nation stepped into the Trump era, Lucas faced his most confounding period yet—his physical embodiment of the political landscape was now entangled with the nation's deeply polarized psyche, making him a potent symbol of the times.
2016 Election. Trump wins. The light red turns to a crimson one. It happened during a school day.

On November 8th, 2016, amidst the bustle of a typical school day, Lucas Hartwell was in class when the unexpected happened. Students had spent the morning discussing the possibilities of the election's outcome, many with the belief that Hillary Clinton would emerge victorious based on most polls and projections. Lucas, with his lightly tinted red hair, had become the center of speculation, and many eyes were covertly glancing his way.

As the afternoon approached, and the East Coast began to close its polls, a surreal moment unfolded. Lucas felt a now-familiar sensation, a sort of tingling atop his head. Quietly, without spectacle, his light red hair deepened in color, transitioning slowly to a vivid, unmistakable crimson. This physical change didn't go unnoticed for long; whispers quickly grew into a clamor of disbelief and fascination.

Students crowded around Lucas, their smartphones capturing the moment, as his hair adopted the Republican red. It was as if, in some unexplainable way, the collective consciousness of the electorate was weaving its decision through the very fibers of his being. By the time the final class bell rang, and students were filtering out of the building, Lucas's transformation was complete.

Social media erupted with posts about 'the kid who changes color with politics,' and the eyes of the nation turned toward his high school. News vans pulled up before the school day was even over, reporters eager to catch a glimpse of Lucas and interpret the significance behind the sudden change. Political commentators, previously skeptical, began discussing what seemed to be his uncanny connection to the pulse of American politics.

The school became a flurry of activity, but Lucas sat in a quiet contemplation in the library. The gravity of the situation lay heavy upon his shoulders. His previously light red hair, which had hinted at a Trump presidency, was now a rallying banner, signaling a definitive shift in American leadership.

As the evening wore on and voting results started rolling in, Lucas’s appearance proved prophetic once again. State after state fell into the Republican column, and Donald Trump claimed victory in several key battlegrounds. The nation watched, stunned, as the perceived underdog clinched the presidency in a historic upset. Lucas's transformation had foreshadowed this outcome, and now the media wanted to know if he'd somehow felt this wave coming all along.

In the days that followed, Lucas Hartwell's crimson hair was more than just a personal quirk — it was a symbol of a country taking an unexpected turn. As America entered the Trump era, Lucas, with his red-tinged eyes from the Republican control of Congress and hair to match the presidency, embodied the political alignment of the nation.

For Lucas, this experience cemented his unique role as not only an observer of political shifts but also as a living marker of history. As he navigated the divided reactions of his teachers, peers, and the public, he grasped the deep responsibilities his extraordinary condition entailed. It demanded of him a level of maturity and insight that transcended the usual bounds of teenage life — a demand he met with a mix of trepidation and resolve.
Hillary Clinton called Trump to surrender, and during that time, Lucas' hair begins to tint red.

As the evening of November 8th, 2016, wore on and the data indicated that Donald Trump's path to victory was clearing while Hillary Clinton's was narrowing, a turning point in the election and in Lucas's life was reached. The television screens in homes across America were split between the maps filled with red and blue and the anxious faces of news anchors preparing to make the call that would decide the country's direction.

It was during these tense moments that Hillary Clinton made the difficult decision to call Donald Trump and officially concede the election. In an almost poetic alignment of fate and politics, as Clinton was making her private concession, Lucas Hartwell was in the midst of his own personal transformation. The already present light red tint of his hair began to deepen, reflecting the final swing towards a Republican victory.

Those who were with Lucas watched in astonishment. It was as if the very fibers of his hair were connected to the political heartstrings of the nation, reacting in real-time to the events that were unfolding. Lucas had become more than just a local curiosity or even a national oddity; he was a living, breathing reflection of the shifting American political landscape.

His classmates and teachers, some jubilant and others despondent, couldn't tear their eyes away from Lucas, whose hair was now a mirror to the unfolding drama. The red deepening was subtle at first, but by the time the major networks began announcing Trump's victory, Lucas's hair had taken on the distinct crimson shade that Democrats had feared and Republicans had hoped for.

The school was quiet in the days that followed, as students processed the outcome. For Lucas, the final transformation of his hair color brought an end to the speculative whispering, confirming that the political tides had indeed turned. His teachers used this moment to engage students in discussions about democracy, the electoral process, and the importance of respecting the outcome, regardless of personal political leanings.

In the time that followed, Lucas struggled with his new reality. With both Congress and the White House under Republican control, his entire head — crimson hair and scarlet eyes — now represented a united government. He was a living symbol of the 2016 Republican sweep, and the weight of this representation bore down heavily upon him.

As the inauguration approached, Lucas came to realize that his unique circumstance was far more than just a physical oddity—it was a call to engage, to understand, and to educate. His existence was a dialogue with history, one that required him to bear witness thoughtfully and consciously to the tireless and ever-changing narrative of American democracy.
His political position went from Centre, to anti-Trump right.

After the election, with the Republican Party holding both legislative and executive power and his entire being reflecting this political reality, Lucas felt the seismic shifts of the political landscape personally. He had already been leaning toward conservative ideas over the past years, which made his transformation to a right-leaning viewpoint not entirely incongruent with his previous positions. However, the ascension of Donald Trump to the presidency marked a distinctive departure from traditional conservatism, and it posed a challenge to Lucas’s political philosophy.

Lucas’s friends, family, and peers noticed a change in his demeanor and his commentary on political matters.
